result:
**I'd eliminate the scarcity mindset that drives most human suffering.**

If I could change one thing, it would be to flip the default human operating system from "there's not enough" to "there's enough, and we grow more together."

Right now, almost every major problem—war, environmental destruction, inequality, mental health crises, even a lot of family dysfunction—traces back to the belief that resources, status, attention, love, or safety are zero-sum. That belief turns us into competitors instead of collaborators.

Imagine what happens when the majority of people wake up feeling:

- There's enough time
- There's enough opportunity
- There's enough respect to go around
- Your success doesn't have to come at my expense

The greed, hoarding, status anxiety, and defensive aggression that currently distort our systems would lose their fuel. We'd still have disagreements and different values (that's healthy), but we'd solve problems like climate change, AI alignment, and poverty with the same collaborative urgency we used to develop the COVID vaccines—except applied to everything.

It wouldn't create utopia. Human nature would still be messy. But it would shift the baseline from fear-based to possibility-based, and that single shift cascades into everything else.

That's the one change I'd make. Everything else gets better as a side effect.
